title: Prefer $derived over $effect for computed values
description: Computed state should be declared as derived, reserving effects for genuine side effects to avoid feedback loops.
body: Use when a value is a pure function of other state. Do not write a $effect that reads state and then writes other state to 'compute' it. Actions: declare computed values with $derived (or legacy $: assignment) so they recompute lazily and cannot self-trigger; reserve $effect for true side effects like DOM measurement or external sync. Validation signals include a test confirming the computed value updates with its inputs and that no effect re-runs unboundedly. Risk boundary: a $effect that writes the state it depends on creates an infinite update loop; if computation needs an effect, gate it and never feed its output back into its own dependencies.
